title: Abnormal Pap Smear - Cervical Cancer
chief_complaint: 42-year-old G3P3 with abnormal Pap smear showing HSIL. Colposcopy with biopsy shows CIN3/CIS. HPV 16 positive. No symptoms. No postcoital bleeding.

physical_exam.pelvic: Cervix appears normal, no visible lesions, no parametrial involvement

hidden_data.cone_biopsy: Microinvasive squamous cell carcinoma, depth 2mm, width 5mm, LVSI negative

key_learning_points: Stage IA1: depth <3mm, width <7mm, LVSI negative
Treatment: cone biopsy or simple hysterectomy if fertility not desired

patient_responses.pap: I haven't had a Pap smear in 5 years

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Stage IA1 - excellent prognosis.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is microinvasive cervical cancer. The cone biopsy confirms it.
feedback.attending_note: Stage IA1: Simple hysterectomy (if fertility not desired) or cone biopsy with negative margins (if fertility desired). Excellent prognosis - 5-year survival >99%. HPV vaccination for children.
